title: Mortality
description: Canonical mortality model for Polis substrate: causes of death, needs-calibration parameters used by MortalityTickWorkflow, and the death-event record written when is_alive flips false. V0 mortality severity = PERMISSIVE (deaths are rare; mostly acute or long-arc chronic; no everyday starvation). Suicide is CATALOGUED but ENGINE-GATED — engine refuses to write death_event.cause = suicide unless an authored workflow with explicit safeguarding_signoff_id passes through. See polis/MORTALITY-DESIGN.md.

death_cause.description: The catalogued cause-of-death taxonomy. Engine refuses any cause not in this enum. Adding new causes is a schema PR (Tier 2 contribution per OPEN-SOURCE.md). The 'suicide' cause is engine-gated — see suicide_safeguarding_policy below.

death_event.description: Record written when a character dies. Null while is_alive = true. Populated atomically by MortalityTickWorkflow (chronic) or by an acute-cause workflow (sport injury, accident, violence). Triggers the CharacterDeathEvent saga: contracts terminate, estate distributes per Foxbridge Property Act §5, institution-role-vacate, succession opens.

lot_id.description: Where it happened. Null if unknown / off-substrate.

ruling_institution_id.description: Coroner / Foxbridge Police / Federation tribunal that issued findings. Null while findings pending.

narrative.description: Public-facing prose summary (e.g. 'collapsed during the 67th minute of the Combination match vs Greycombe; pronounced at Hartford General; coroner found undiagnosed cardiomyopathy.').

supporting_evidence_uris.description: Pointers to coroner report, match-incident log, conflict-record, etc. Substrate-tracked.

estate_distributed_at_in_world.description: Set when the estate cascade completes. Null until then.

safeguarding_signoff_id.description: REQUIRED if cause = suicide. Foreign key to a Foxbridge-authority signoff record. Engine refuses cause: suicide without it. Reason: substrate-level harm-reduction floor; see polis/MORTALITY-DESIGN.md §safeguarding.

vital_status.description: Lifetime mortality bookkeeping for a character. Set partly at spawn (life_expectancy_age, biological_age_modifier from country actuarial table + lifestyle) and updated over time as chronic conditions accrue.

life_expectancy_age.description: Expected age at natural death. Sampled at spawn from country.actuarial_life_expectancy distribution by sex/cohort, plus character-specific modifiers. After this age, MortalityTickWorkflow rolls increasing natural_age probability per year.

biological_age_modifier.description: Lifestyle / occupation / health adjustment to life_expectancy. Negative = lives longer (athletic baseline = -2 to -4; clean diet = -1). Positive = lives shorter (heavy smoker +5; long career as professional boxer +3; chronic condition adds +1 to +8).

chronic_conditions.description: Ongoing conditions affecting morbidity/mortality. Each is a foreign key to a (substrate-fictional) condition catalogue at country level (V1+; at V0 free-form strings).

treating_institution_id.description: FNHS or private clinic providing care. Null if untreated.

expected_terminal_in_world.description: Set only for severity: terminal. MortalityTickWorkflow rolls illness_terminal cause as this date approaches.

death_event.description: Null while is_alive = true. Populated atomically when is_alive flips false.

needs_calibration.description: World-level decay-and-restore parameters per need dimension. Applied uniformly to all characters in the world; per-character offsets via lifestyle / wealth / institution-role. Per PRD §10.2. The MortalityTickWorkflow consumes these parameters to translate sustained needs-deprivation into health damage and ultimately mortality.

decay_per_day.description: Fraction of need value lost per in-world day with no consumption. E.g. hunger 0.30 = full meal sustains ~3 days at most before refilling needed; thirst 0.50 = half-day.

seek_threshold.description: Below this value the character actively seeks restoration (shopping, eating, sleeping, etc). Behaviour-tree trigger; not LLM-gated for routine cases.

damage_floor.description: Below this value sustained deprivation begins to damage health.

days_at_floor_to_health_damage.description: Days at or below damage_floor before each subsequent in-world day reduces health by the dimension-specific health_damage_per_day_below_floor amount.

health_damage_per_day_below_floor.description: How much health is lost per day below floor after the grace period.

seek_threshold.description: Below this value character seeks medical care (FNHS / private clinic / self-medication).

damage_floor.description: Below this value other needs decay faster (compounding spiral).

mortality_threshold.description: Below this value MortalityTickWorkflow begins rolling weekly illness_untreated death probability.

weekly_mortality_probability_at_zero_hea.description: Weekly probability of death at health = 0. Linearly interpolated from 0 at mortality_threshold up to this value at health = 0. Permissive default keeps rate low even at floor.

actuarial_life_expectancy.description: Country-level life-expectancy distribution. Used at character spawn to sample life_expectancy_age. Substrate-fictional, but approximates real-world bands at calibration time.

v0_severity.description: Engine global. permissive = V0 default; deaths are rare and mostly acute or long-arc chronic. off = schemas in place but no character actually dies (CI / preview environments). realistic = full PRD §10.2-§10.3 needs-floor mortality active. Switchable by founder per world.

suicide_safeguarding_policy.description: engine_gated_v0 = engine refuses to write death_event.cause = suicide without explicit safeguarding_signoff_id from an authored workflow. LLM-generated narratives proposing suicide are refused at write-time, regardless of how plausible the in-world arc looks. Substrate-level harm-reduction floor; see polis/MORTALITY-DESIGN.md §safeguarding.
